name_romaji: Tomihara Chiku Jigami Oyobi Yama no Kami
name_en: Earth Deity and Mountain Deity of the Tomihara District
description_en: This is a monument (historic site) consisting of the earth deity (jigami) and mountain deity (yama no kami) of the Tomihara district. It is a town-designated cultural property of Kamifurano Town, designated on 1980-12-09, located at Higashi 7-sen Kita 24-go, Kamifurano Town.

This is a tangible cultural property (structure) consisting of the Kamifurano Town Folk Museum. It is a town-designated cultural property of Kamifurano Town, designated on 2022-01-24, located at Tomimachi 1-chome 3-30, Kamifurano Town.

This is a tangible cultural property (structure) consisting of the Kamifurano Town Pioneer Memorial Hall. It is a town-designated cultural property of Kamifurano Town, designated on 2022-01-24, located at Nishi 3-sen Kita 28-go, Kamifurano Town.

This is a tangible cultural property (fine art and crafts) consisting of paintings by Goto Sumio. It is a town-designated cultural property of Kamifurano Town, designated on 2022-01-24, located at Higashi 4-sen Kita 21-go, Kamifurano Town.

name_en: Marimo Moss Balls of Lake Kussharo
description_en: This is a monument (natural monument) consisting of the marimo moss balls of Lake Kussharo. It is a town-designated cultural property of Teshikaga Town, designated on 1969-07-11.

This is an intangible cultural property (folk performing art) consisting of the Tsukembetsu lion dance (shishimai). It is a town-designated cultural property of Teshikaga Town, designated on 1971-07-31.
This is an intangible cultural property (folk performing art) consisting of the Nita lion dance (shishimai). It is a town-designated cultural property of Teshikaga Town, designated on 1971-07-31.
This is an intangible cultural property (folk performing art) consisting of the Kawayu festival music (bayashi). It is a town-designated cultural property of Teshikaga Town, designated on 2025-02-26.
This is a monument (historic site, scenic beauty, natural monument) consisting of the founding pine of Nishibetsu. It is a town-designated cultural property of Betsukai Town.
This is a monument (historic site, scenic beauty, natural monument) consisting of the Kurile cherry tree of Notsuke. It is a town-designated cultural property of Betsukai Town.

name_en: Cherry Tree of the Experimental Station
description_en: This is a monument (historic site, scenic beauty, natural monument) consisting of the cherry tree at the experimental station. It is a town-designated cultural property of Betsukai Town.
This is a monument (historic site, scenic beauty, natural monument) consisting of the great oak (nara) of Okuyukiusu. It is a town-designated cultural property of Betsukai Town.

name_romaji: Fūren no Narabayashi / Kaikon Kinen no Sumomo
name_en: Furen Oak Grove and Plum of Land-Reclamation Commemoration
description_en: This is a monument (historic site, scenic beauty, natural monument) consisting of the Furen oak grove and the plum tree commemorating land reclamation. It is a town-designated cultural property of Betsukai Town.
This is a monument (historic site, scenic beauty, natural monument) consisting of Okuyukiusu station. It is a town-designated cultural property of Betsukai Town.
This is a monument (historic site, scenic beauty, natural monument) consisting of the pine tree of Royama. It is a town-designated cultural property of Betsukai Town.
This is a monument (historic site, scenic beauty, natural monument) consisting of the lone pine of Hombetsukai. It is a town-designated cultural property of Betsukai Town.

name_en: Oak of the Town Office Branch
description_en: This is a monument (historic site, scenic beauty, natural monument) consisting of the oak (kashiwa) at the town office branch. It is a town-designated cultural property of Betsukai Town.

name_romaji: Kyū Betsukai Son'ei Kidō Fūrensen Okuyukiusu Teiryūjo
name_en: Okuyukiusu Stop on the Former Betsukai Village Railway Furen Line
description_en: This is a monument (historic site, scenic beauty, natural monument) consisting of the Okuyukiusu stop on the former Betsukai village-operated railway Furen Line. It is a town-designated cultural property of Betsukai Town.

name_en: Rokki Oak of the Hirono Reclamation
description_en: This is a monument (historic site, scenic beauty, natural monument) consisting of the Rokki oak (kashiwa) of the Hirono land reclamation. It is a town-designated cultural property of Betsukai Town.
This is a monument (historic site, scenic beauty, natural monument) consisting of the oak (kashiwa) at Kaminishibetsu Elementary School. It is a town-designated cultural property of Betsukai Town.
This is a tangible cultural property consisting of documents of the Kaga family. It is a town-designated cultural property of Betsukai Town.

name_en: Mammoth Fossil Cluster off the Notsuke Peninsula
description_en: This is a monument (historic site, scenic beauty, natural monument) consisting of a cluster of mammoth fossils found off the Notsuke Peninsula. It is a town-designated cultural property of Betsukai Town.

name_romaji: Zushiiri Minamiyausubetsu Batō Kanzeon Bosatsu Zazō
name_en: Seated Bato-Kannon Statue of Minamiyausubetsu in a Portable Shrine
description_en: This is a tangible cultural property consisting of a seated statue of Bato-Kannon (Hayagriva Avalokitesvara) of Minamiyausubetsu, enshrined in a portable shrine (zushi). It is a town-designated cultural property of Betsukai Town.

name_en: Repository of the Former Kashiwano Ordinary Elementary School
description_en: This is a tangible cultural property consisting of the hoanden (imperial portrait repository) of the former Kashiwano ordinary elementary school. It is a town-designated cultural property of Betsukai Town.

name_en: Site of the Hiyoriyama Signal-Fire Platform at Ogon Cape
description_en: This is a monument (historic site) marking the site of the Hiyoriyama signal-fire platform at Ogon Cape. It is a municipally designated cultural property of Rumoi City, designated on 1999-09-27, located at Omachi 2-chome, Rumoi City (marker post installed in fiscal 1989).

This is a monument (historic site) consisting of the Sandomari fishing port site. It is a municipally designated cultural property of Rumoi City, designated on 1999-09-27, located at Sandomari-cho, Rumoi City (marker post installed in fiscal 1990).

This is a tangible cultural property consisting of a fragment of a hoshikabuto (star helmet). It is a municipally designated cultural property of Rumoi City, designated on 1999-09-27, located at Omachi 2-chome, Rumoi City (on display at the Umi no Furusatokan).

This is a tangible cultural property consisting of two attush garments woven from tree-bark fiber. It is a municipally designated cultural property of Rumoi City, designated on 1999-09-27, located at Omachi 2-chome, Rumoi City (Umi no Furusatokan; not currently open to the public).

name_en: Site of the Former Igarashi Okutaro Residence
description_en: This is a monument (historic site) marking the site of the former Igarashi Okutaro residence. It is a municipally designated cultural property of Rumoi City, designated on 2017-05-16, located within Omachi 3-chome 37-2, Rumoi City.

name_romaji: Tsujimurake Garasu Kanban Shashin Oyobi Kanren Shiryō
name_en: Tsujimura Family Glass-Plate Photographs and Related Materials
description_en: This is a tangible cultural property consisting of glass-plate photographs and related materials of the Tsujimura family. It is a municipally designated cultural property of Iwamizawa City, designated on 2025-12-24.

name_romaji: Sairei Dashi Ebisuyama Ningyō Omensō Oyobi Fuzokuhin
name_en: Face and Accessories of the Ebisuyama Festival Float Figure
description_en: This is a folk cultural property (tangible) consisting of the figure's face and accessories of the Ebisuyama festival float. It is a town-designated cultural property of Esashi Town, designated on 1999-04-19.

name_romaji: Sairei Dashi Hōnenyama Mizuhiki Karako Shunjū no Zu
name_en: Chinese Children in Spring and Autumn Hanging on the Honenyama Festival Float
description_en: This is a folk cultural property (tangible) consisting of the decorative hanging depicting Chinese children in spring and autumn scenes on the Honenyama festival float. It is a town-designated cultural property of Esashi Town, designated on 1999-04-19.

name_romaji: Sairei Dashi Hōnenyama Mizuhiki Nihon Sankei no Zu
name_en: Three Views of Japan Hanging on the Honenyama Festival Float
description_en: This is a folk cultural property (tangible) consisting of the decorative hanging depicting the Three Views of Japan on the Honenyama festival float. It is a town-designated cultural property of Esashi Town, designated on 1999-04-19.

name_romaji: Sairei Dashi Shōmuyama Ningyō Omensō Oyobi Fuzokuhin
name_en: Face and Accessories of the Shomuyama Festival Float Figure
description_en: This is a folk cultural property (tangible) consisting of the figure's face and accessories of the Shomuyama festival float. It is a town-designated cultural property of Esashi Town, designated on 1999-04-19.
This is a folk cultural property (intangible) consisting of the Esashi Doba deer dance (shishimai). It is a town-designated cultural property of Esashi Town, designated on 1973-02-05.
This is a folk cultural property (intangible) consisting of the Esashi Tazawa deer dance (shishimai). It is a town-designated cultural property of Esashi Town, designated on 1973-02-05.
This is a folk cultural property (intangible) consisting of the Esashi deer dance (shishimai). It is a town-designated cultural property of Esashi Town, designated on 1973-02-05.
This is a folk cultural property (intangible) consisting of the Esashi shark dance (same odori). It is a town-designated cultural property of Esashi Town, designated on 1991-11-26.
This is a folk cultural property (intangible) consisting of the Esashi Oiwake dance. It is a town-designated cultural property of Esashi Town, designated on 1995-06-13.

name_romaji: Boshin no Eki Senshisha no Funbo
name_en: Graves of Those Killed in the Boshin War
description_en: This is a monument (historic site) consisting of the graves of those killed in the Boshin War. It is a town-designated cultural property of Esashi Town, designated on 1971-12-05.
